name: Assam
landmark: Kaziranga's one-horned rhinoceros and the Brahmaputra river-island culture of Majuli
stopoverNote: Guwahati (GAU) is the main flight and rail gateway, with Jorhat useful for Majuli/Sivasagar and Dibrugarh for upper Assam. Assam itself does not require an Inner Line Permit, but neighbouring Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland and Mizoram do; carry ID for hotels, ferries and safari bookings. Internal travel is road-heavy, with Brahmaputra ferries for Majuli and long transfers between wildlife parks.

diet.vegetarian: Vegetarian travel is practical in Guwahati, Jorhat/Sivasagar and most resorts: ask for Assamese veg thali with rice, dal, aloo pitika, khar made with papaya or bottle gourd, xaak bhaji, tomato/ou tenga and pitha. In Kaziranga, Manas and Majuli homestays, pre-request vegetarian meals because fixed thalis often default to fish or meat sides.
diet.vegan: Vegan is manageable but needs clear requests. Many Assamese vegetable dishes use mustard oil and are naturally dairy-free, but thalis may include curd, ghee, paneer, egg, fish stock, fermented fish chutney or meat/fish cooked in the same kitchen. Remote island and park stays can have fixed menus, so request oil-only cooking, no dairy and no fish/meat when booking.
phrases: Moi niramis kham, maas-mangxo-nidibo = I eat vegetarian food, please do not give fish or meat
Dudh, ghee, paneer aru doi nidibo = please do not add milk, ghee, paneer or curd
Telot randhibo, maasor jhol nalage = please cook in oil; I do not want fish gravy

links.label: Kaziranga National Park safari timings and rates

cities.name: Guwahati
cities.notes: Assam's practical gateway for Kamakhya Temple, Brahmaputra cruises, museums, markets and onward road or rail connections across the state.

cities.name: Kaziranga National Park
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed floodplain wilderness and India's classic one-horned rhino safari base, best planned around Kohora/Bagori with early starts.

cities.name: Majuli
cities.notes: Brahmaputra river-island culture base for satras, mask-making, Mising villages, cycling and slow homestay travel reached by ferry from the Jorhat side.

cities.name: Sivasagar / Jorhat
cities.notes: Upper Assam heritage-and-tea base pairing Ahom-era Sivasagar monuments with Jorhat logistics, tea estates and the Nimati ferry approach to Majuli.

cities.name: Manas National Park
cities.notes: Less-crowded UNESCO wildlife base on the Bhutan foothills, with jeep safaris, birding, village stays and Manas River landscapes.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel work well in Guwahati, Jorhat and Sivasagar; coverage is patchier inside Kaziranga/Manas ranges, on Majuli interiors and along river/forest roads. Download maps, keep ferry/safari contacts offline, and carry cash for islands, village stays and park gates.

Guwahati.name: Guwahati
Guwahati.note: Two days gives one temple-and-river gateway day plus one city-culture day before the long Kaziranga road transfer.

days.note: Use this as a soft landing after the flight/train. Kamakhya queues can be long, and evening river cruises are weather-dependent, so avoid stacking too much on arrival day.

activities.name: Kamakhya Temple on Nilachal Hill
activities.tip: Go early for general darshan; paid/special access rules change during festivals, so verify only with the official temple counter/site. Ambubachi in late June is extremely crowded and falls in the monsoon avoid window.

activities.name: Umananda Island ferry and temple
activities.tip: Ferries depend on river conditions; skip if the Brahmaputra is running high.

activities.name: Brahmaputra sunset cruise from Uzan Bazar/Jahajghat
activities.tip: 2026 public fares vary by deck and dinner package; book ahead in peak winter and verify departure jetty.

activities.name: Assamese thali dinner
activities.tip: Ask for veg khar, aloo pitika, xaak and tomato/ou tenga if avoiding fish.

hotels.name: Uzan Bazar or Khanapara mid-range hotel

meals: Veg Assamese thali with aloo pitika

days.theme: Museums, markets and northeast gateway logistics
days.note: Keep the sightseeing within central Guwahati so you can also confirm Kaziranga safari timing, driver details and onward luggage plans.

activities.name: Assam State Museum
activities.tip: Useful primer for Ahom, tribal and textile history before Sivasagar/Majuli.

activities.name: Navagraha Temple or Basistha Ashram
Fancy Bazaar and Pan Bazaar walk
activities.tip: Buy tea, gamocha and snacks here; lanes are busy, so keep valuables secure.

activities.name: Kaziranga safari booking and ID check
activities.tip: Carry photo ID for every traveller; elephant safari seats are limited and often confirmed only close to the date.

hotels.name: Uzan Bazar or Khanapara mid-range hotel

meals: Doi chira if you take dairy

Kaziranga National Park.name: Kaziranga National Park
Kaziranga National Park.note: Three days covers the Guwahati transfer, two safari windows and a lighter culture/nature buffer; park operations are usually November–April and monsoon closures/floods are normal.

days.note: The 4.5–5.5 hour road transfer is the day's main content. Arrive before dark, check into the resort belt and reconfirm next-morning safari zone and pickup.

activities.name: Drive Guwahati to Kaziranga via Nagaon
activities.tip: Roadworks, rain and animal-corridor speed controls can slow the drive.

activities.name: Kaziranga Orchid and Biodiversity Park
activities.tip: A good non-safari introduction to Assamese dance, crafts and food if you arrive by afternoon.

activities.name: Safari permit, guide and pickup confirmation
activities.tip: Keep original ID handy; timings and zones can change by season and forest orders.

hotels.name: Kohora budget lodge or eco camp

days.note: Do one early wildlife window and one afternoon window rather than chasing every range. Morning light and cooler hours are best for rhino, elephant, buffalo and bird sightings.

activities.name: Jeep safari in Central or Western range
activities.tip: 2026 rates vary by range; official timings list 7:30–10:00am and 1:30–3:00pm slots.

activities.name: Bagori/Kohora viewpoint and interpretation stop
activities.tip: Ask your guide to prioritise safe distance from rhinos and elephants.

activities.name: Elephant safari if ethically comfortable and seats are available
activities.tip: Limited early slots; verify welfare practices and current forest-department policy before booking.

activities.name: Afternoon jeep safari in a different range
activities.tip: Choose Agoratoli for birds/open wetlands if you already did Central/Bagori.

hotels.name: Kohora budget lodge or eco camp

days.theme: Eastern range birds, Kakochang or transfer buffer
days.note: Use the day for a second distinct habitat, nearby waterfall/tea landscapes, or as a weather buffer before the long Jorhat/Majuli move.

activities.name: Agoratoli/Eastern range birding safari
activities.tip: Best for birders and quieter wetland sightings; it is farther from central Kohora hotels.

activities.name: Kakochang Waterfalls or tea-garden viewpoint
activities.tip: Skip in heavy rain; paths can be slippery.

activities.name: Karbi/Assamese cultural evening at resort
Prepare early road transfer toward Jorhat/Nimati
activities.tip: Majuli ferries stop by late afternoon, so do not leave packing/logistics to the morning.

hotels.name: Kohora budget lodge or eco camp

Majuli.name: Majuli
Majuli.note: Two days is enough for the ferry, satras and village/cycling rhythm; add a third for Raas festival or a slower homestay stay.

days.theme: Nimati ferry, satras and island arrival
days.note: Kaziranga to Nimati plus ferry makes this a transfer-heavy day. Catch a morning/early-afternoon ferry and keep the island sightseeing close to Kamalabari/Garamur.

activities.name: Drive Kaziranga to Nimati Ghat near Jorhat
activities.tip: Start early; ferry timings can shift with Brahmaputra levels.

activities.name: Nimati–Kamalabari Majuli ferry
activities.tip: Recent public guides list daytime ferries roughly 7:30am–4:00pm; monsoon suspensions are common.

activities.name: Auniati Satra or Dakhinpat Satra
activities.tip: Dress modestly and ask before photographing monks, manuscripts or prayer halls.

activities.name: Garamur village and sunset by the wetlands

days.note: Distances are flat but spread out; hire cycles/scooters locally and avoid riding after dark on unlit embankment roads.

activities.name: Samaguri Satra mask-making workshop
activities.tip: Call ahead during festival season because artists may be rehearsing or travelling.

activities.name: Mising village weaving and stilt-house walk
activities.tip: Use a local guide or homestay host rather than entering homes uninvited.

activities.name: Island cycling loop
activities.tip: Carry water and sun protection; roads can be broken after floods.

activities.name: Kamalabari Satra music/dance if available

Sivasagar / Jorhat.name: Sivasagar / Jorhat
Sivasagar / Jorhat.note: Two days lets you return from Majuli, cover the Ahom monuments without rushing, and use Jorhat as the onward rail/air/road base.

days.theme: Majuli ferry return and Jorhat tea-town pause
days.note: The ferry and road return can absorb the morning, so keep the day around Jorhat rather than forcing Sivasagar monuments immediately.

activities.name: Morning ferry Majuli to Nimati and transfer to Jorhat
activities.tip: Do not rely on the last ferry if you have a same-day train or flight.

activities.name: Dhekiakhowa Bornamghar
Jorhat tea-estate or Tocklai Tea Research area drive-by
activities.tip: Formal factory access changes by estate; ask your hotel to arrange a verified visit.

activities.name: Jorhat market snack walk

days.note: Sivasagar's main monuments are clusterable around the tanks and palace sites. Start early from Jorhat or sleep in Sivasagar to avoid midday heat.

activities.name: Rang Ghar royal amphitheatre
activities.tip: Recent visitor reports mention QR/online ticketing and free entry for children up to 15; confirm at the gate.

activities.name: Talatal Ghar palace complex
activities.tip: A local guide helps explain the Ahom military architecture.

activities.name: Sivadol, Vishnudol and Devidol by Sivasagar Tank
Kareng Ghar / Garhgaon Palace extension
activities.tip: Add only if you have a private cab; it sits outside the tight Sivasagar tank cluster.

Manas National Park.name: Manas National Park
Manas National Park.note: Two days works as a focused wildlife extension from Guwahati; add a third only for slower birding, rafting conditions or a village stay buffer.

days.theme: Upper Assam to Manas via Guwahati/Bansbari
days.note: This is a long repositioning leg if coming from Jorhat/Sivasagar; many travellers break in Guwahati. Keep the day to travel, check-in and riverside orientation.

activities.name: Train/flight/road reposition toward Guwahati and Bansbari
activities.tip: If driving from Guwahati, Bansbari is usually 4–5 hours; avoid night arrival on rural roads.

activities.name: Manas resort check-in and safari booking confirmation
activities.tip: Official portal slots can sell out in winter weekends; carry ID for every traveller.

activities.name: Manas River sunset walk near Bansbari
activities.tip: Stay outside restricted forest areas without a guide.

days.theme: Manas jeep safari and river-edge wildlife
days.note: Plan one jeep safari plus a low-key afternoon rather than overscheduling. Manas is quieter than Kaziranga but distances inside and around the reserve are meaningful.

activities.name: Manas jeep safari in Bansbari or Bhuyanpara zone
activities.tip: 2026 official slots are typically 6:30–9:30am, 10:00am–1:00pm and 2:00–5:00pm; book through the official portal where possible.

activities.name: Elephant safari if available and ethically acceptable
activities.tip: Elephant safaris are limited and may be closed on Tuesdays; verify current rules.

activities.name: Bodo village/craft interaction outside the park
activities.tip: Use a local guide and pay directly for crafts or performances.

activities.name: Manas River rafting/boat option if running
activities.tip: Seasonal water levels decide operations; never attempt during flood advisories.

connections.method: Private cab or tourist coach via Nagaon to Kohora/Bagori
Road to Nimati Ghat near Jorhat, then government/private ferry to Kamalabari
Ferry back to Nimati, then road via Jorhat toward Sivasagar
Long rail/flight/road reposition via Guwahati, then road to Bansbari

extras: Assam does not require an Inner Line Permit for normal tourism, but neighbouring Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland and Mizoram do; do not extrapolate Assam rules to onward Northeast travel.
Avoid June–September for leisure trips: Brahmaputra floods disrupt Majuli ferries, Kaziranga/Manas safaris close or restrict movement, and road/rail delays are common.
Kaziranga and Manas safari prices, zones and timings can change by forest order; use official portals/range offices and verify locally before quoting final costs to travellers.
Majuli ferry timings and vehicle capacity change with river levels. Do not plan last-ferry arrivals, and keep a Jorhat buffer if you have a same-day train or flight.
Foreign tourists do not need ILP for Assam but should follow current visa/FRRO rules and hotel registration requirements; carry passport and visa copies.
